model_narrative: We are loading boxes into a single rectangular container. For each box we decide whether to place it in the container at all, and if we do, which orientation to rotate it into and where to position it along each axis. The goal is to make the total volume of the boxes we manage to fit into the container as large as possible.

sets.name: b
sets.doc: the boxes available to be packed into the container

sets.name: o
sets.doc: the six possible orientations in which a box can be rotated, each one a different assignment of the box's three side lengths to the x, y and z axes

sets.name: d
sets.doc: the three spatial axes of the container

sets.name: bo
sets.doc: the box-orientation pairs that are actually allowed; a box may only be rotated into one of the orientations listed for it here, and orientations not listed for a box are unavailable to that box

params.name: dim_o
params.doc: the side length a box would occupy along each axis if it were placed in a given orientation, in centimetres

params.name: box_vol
params.doc: the volume of each box, in cubic metres

params.name: dim_cont
params.doc: the interior length of the container along each axis, in centimetres

vars.name: OMEGA
vars.doc: placement indicator for a box; 1 if the box is placed inside the container and 0 if it is left out

vars.name: ALPHA
vars.doc: orientation-selection indicator defined only over the allowed box-orientation pairs; 1 if the box is placed in that orientation and 0 otherwise

vars.name: RELPOS
vars.doc: relative-position indicator for an ordered pair of boxes along an axis; 1 if the first box is positioned before the second box along that axis, meaning the first box's far edge along that axis is at or below the second box's near edge

vars.name: LOC
vars.doc: the coordinate of the near corner of a box along each axis, in centimetres, measured from the container origin

vars.name: DIM
vars.doc: the realized side length a box occupies along each axis once its orientation is chosen, in centimetres

vars.name: VOL
vars.doc: the total volume of all boxes placed in the container, in cubic metres

constraints.name: DIM[b,
constraints.intent: For each box and each axis, the side length the box takes up along that axis must match the side length it would have in whichever orientation was chosen for it. Only the orientations that are allowed for that box can contribute, and exactly the one that is selected determines the side length along that axis.

constraints.name: ALPHA[b,
constraints.intent: For each box, an orientation is chosen exactly when the box is placed in the container. If the box is placed, precisely one of its allowed orientations must be selected, and if the box is left out, none of its orientations may be selected.

constraints.name: LOC[b,
constraints.intent: For each box and each axis, a placed box must lie entirely within the container along that axis, so its near corner plus the side length it occupies cannot reach beyond the container's interior length on that axis. A box that is not placed takes up no room along any axis.

constraints.name: b.ord(b1)
constraints.intent: For each unordered pair of distinct boxes and each axis, at most one of the two boxes can be positioned before the other along that axis, and only when the boxes are actually placed. If a box is left out, no relative-position relationship along any axis may be active for the pairs it belongs to.

constraints.name: b.ord(b1)
constraints.intent: For each unordered pair of distinct boxes, whenever both boxes are placed in the container they must be separated along at least one axis, with one box positioned before the other on that axis. If at most one of the two is placed, no such separation is required.

constraints.name: LOC[b1,
constraints.intent: For each ordered pair of distinct boxes and each axis, if the first box is positioned before the second along that axis then the first box must finish before the second begins, so the first box's near corner plus its side length on that axis cannot exceed the second box's near corner. When the first box is not positioned before the second along that axis the requirement is relaxed and does not bind.

constraints.name: VOL
constraints.intent: The total packed volume equals the combined volume of every box that is placed in the container, counting each placed box's own volume and ignoring boxes left out.

query: Suppose this run only has room on the manifest for 40 cartons total, the rest waiting for the next truck. We're curious what the optimizer keeps loaded — what's the total packed volume if we hold the box count to at most 40?
expected_pyomo: model.cap_count40 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.OMEGA[b] for b in model.b) <= 40)

query: There's a proposal on the table to leave a safety margin on weight, so the loaded cartons shouldn't add up to more than ninety-five percent of the combined volume of everything staged on the dock. We're curious how the load shakes out under that volume ceiling. What total packed volume does the optimizer return?
expected_pyomo: model.vol_budget = Constraint(expr=sum(model.box_vol[b]*model.OMEGA[b] for b in model.b) <= 0.95*sum(value(model.box_vol[b]) for b in model.b))

query: We're looking at a lighter shipment where only the 16 biggest cartons travel in this container. If we cap the load at 16 boxes, what packed volume does the plan report?

query: When I trim the manifest to 30 cartons, the plan throws out every one of the small half-size boxes plus a couple of the mid ones. Shouldn't it be keeping more of the little cartons since they're cheap to slot in? What's the optimizer seeing that makes dropping them the right call?
expected_pyomo: model.cap_count30 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.OMEGA[b] for b in model.b) <= 30)

query: Looking at the pick list, the plan leans hard on the back half of the manifest and crams in nearly every carton numbered in the second half of our roster, while the front-half cartons sit closer to the door for easy access. Shouldn't the load favor the front-half cartons instead? Hold the count of placed back-half cartons to no more than ninety percent of the placed front-half cartons and tell me what that costs us in volume, because I'd expect the tradeoff to be small.
expected_pyomo: model.upper_share = Constraint(expr=sum(model.OMEGA[model.b.at(i)] for i in range(len(model.b)//2+1, len(model.b)+1)) <= 0.9*sum(model.OMEGA[model.b.at(i)] for i in range(1, len(model.b)//2+1)))

constraints.intent: The total packed volume equals the combined volume of every box that is placed in the container, counting each placed box's own volume and ignoring boxes left out.

query: If the dock supervisor insists we ship exactly one carton fewer than a full load this cycle, the model picks off a small box. Why drop a small one rather than spreading the cut? Hold the load to 45 cartons and tell me the resulting volume.
expected_pyomo: model.cap_count45 = Constraint(expr=sum(model.OMEGA[b] for b in model.b) <= 45)
